commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And your children shall wander in the wilderness forty years, and bear your harlotries, until your carcasses are consumed in the wilderness. Within five days from the departure out of Egypt, (Menochius) and above 38 from this time. Hebrew, "they shall be shepherds "without any fixed dwelling, like the shepherds of that country. They had complained that Chanaan consumed or devoured its inhabitants. (Calmet) Their children underwent a temporal, but salutary, punishment for their sin. Augustine, ep. ) (Worthington).

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Shall bear your fornication: That is, shall bear the punishment of your disloyalty to God, which in the scripture language is here called a fornication, in a spiritual sense.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
